kids.question: Who wrote this letter?
kids.option_a: Peter, an apostle of Jesus Christ
kids.explanation: 1 Peter 1:1 — "Peter, an apostle of Jesus Christ, to the strangers scattered throughout Pontus, Galatia, Cappadocia, Asia, and Bithynia."

kids.question: What kind of hope do believers have through Jesus?
kids.option_a: A living hope because Jesus rose from the dead
kids.option_b: A fading hope that might not come true
kids.option_c: A hope only for this life
kids.option_d: A hope based on being good enough
kids.explanation: 1 Peter 1:3 — "Hath begotten us again unto a lively hope by the resurrection of Jesus Christ from the dead."

kids.question: What does Peter say is more precious than gold?
kids.explanation: 1 Peter 1:7 — "That the trial of your faith, being much more precious than of gold that perisheth, though it be tried with fire."

teens.question: How does Peter describe the believers' inheritance?
teens.option_a: Incorruptible, undefiled, and that fadeth not away, reserved in heaven
teens.option_b: Earthly land promised to Abraham's children
teens.option_c: Gold and silver stored in the temple
teens.explanation: 1 Peter 1:4 — "To an inheritance incorruptible, and undefiled, and that fadeth not away, reserved in heaven for you."

teens.question: Why are trials of faith valuable?
teens.option_a: That the trial of your faith might be found unto praise and honour and glory at the appearing of Jesus Christ
teens.option_b: So believers can boast about their suffering
teens.option_c: So God can separate the weak from the strong
teens.option_d: So the world will pity Christians
teens.explanation: 1 Peter 1:7 — "That the trial of your faith, being much more precious than of gold that perisheth, though it be tried with fire, might be found unto praise and honour and glory at the appearing of Jesus Christ."

teens.question: What does Peter say believers should be?
teens.option_a: Holy, for God is holy
teens.option_b: Wealthy, for God owns everything
teens.option_c: Popular, for God loves all people
teens.explanation: 1 Peter 1:15-16 — "But as he which hath called you is holy, so be ye holy in all manner of conversation; Because it is written, Be ye holy; for I am holy."

teens.question: What were believers redeemed with?
teens.option_a: The precious blood of Christ, as of a lamb without blemish and without spot
teens.option_b: Silver and gold from the temple treasury
teens.option_c: Good works and righteous deeds
teens.explanation: 1 Peter 1:18-19 — "Ye were not redeemed with corruptible things, as silver and gold... But with the precious blood of Christ, as of a lamb without blemish and without spot."

teens.question: How long does the word of the Lord endure?
teens.option_a: For ever
teens.option_b: For a thousand years
teens.option_c: Until the end of this age
teens.explanation: 1 Peter 1:25 — "But the word of the Lord endureth for ever. And this is the word which by the gospel is preached unto you."

adults.question: To whom does Peter address this letter?
adults.option_a: Strangers scattered throughout Pontus, Galatia, Cappadocia, Asia, and Bithynia
adults.option_b: The church at Rome
adults.option_c: The twelve tribes of Israel
adults.option_d: The Gentile churches founded by Paul
adults.explanation: 1 Peter 1:1 — "Peter, an apostle of Jesus Christ, to the strangers scattered throughout Pontus, Galatia, Cappadocia, Asia, and Bithynia."

adults.question: What three aspects of the Trinity are mentioned in verse 2?
adults.option_a: Foreknowledge of God the Father, sanctification of the Spirit, sprinkling of the blood of Jesus Christ
adults.option_b: Creation by the Father, incarnation of the Son, indwelling of the Spirit
adults.option_c: Wrath of the Father, sacrifice of the Son, comfort of the Spirit
adults.option_d: Sovereignty of the Father, teaching of the Son, gifts of the Spirit
adults.explanation: 1 Peter 1:2 — "Elect according to the foreknowledge of God the Father, through sanctification of the Spirit, unto obedience and sprinkling of the blood of Jesus Christ."

adults.question: What did the Old Testament prophets search and inquire about?
adults.option_a: What, or what manner of time the Spirit of Christ which was in them did signify
adults.option_b: When the Messiah would overthrow Rome
adults.option_c: How to build the second temple
adults.option_d: Where the lost tribes of Israel had gone
adults.explanation: 1 Peter 1:10-11 — "Of which salvation the prophets have enquired and searched diligently... Searching what, or what manner of time the Spirit of Christ which was in them did signify."

adults.question: What does Peter say even the angels desire?
adults.option_a: To look into the things of the gospel
adults.option_b: To become human and experience salvation
adults.option_c: To worship in the earthly temple
adults.option_d: To rule over earthly kingdoms
adults.explanation: 1 Peter 1:12 — "Which things the angels desire to look into."

adults.question: What does Peter tell believers to gird up?
adults.option_a: The loins of your mind
adults.option_b: Your physical strength
adults.option_c: Your financial resources
adults.option_d: Your political alliances
adults.explanation: 1 Peter 1:13 — "Wherefore gird up the loins of your mind, be sober, and hope to the end for the grace that is to be brought unto you at the revelation of Jesus Christ."

adults.question: How were believers born again according to verse 23?
adults.option_a: Not of corruptible seed, but of incorruptible, by the word of God which liveth and abideth for ever
adults.option_b: By water baptism in the Jordan River
adults.option_c: Through the laying on of apostolic hands
adults.option_d: By circumcision and keeping the law
adults.explanation: 1 Peter 1:23 — "Being born again, not of corruptible seed, but of incorruptible, by the word of God, which liveth and abideth for ever."

adults.question: What does Peter compare human life to in verse 24?
adults.option_a: All flesh is as grass, and all the glory of man as the flower of grass that withereth
adults.option_b: A mighty cedar tree that stands for centuries
adults.option_c: A rock that endures through all storms
adults.option_d: A river that flows without ceasing
adults.explanation: 1 Peter 1:24 — "For all flesh is as grass, and all the glory of man as the flower of grass. The grass withereth, and the flower thereof falleth away."

adults.question: What does Peter say about believers' relationship with Christ whom they have not seen?
adults.option_a: Whom having not seen, ye love; in whom ye rejoice with joy unspeakable and full of glory
adults.option_b: Whom not seeing, ye doubt and question
adults.option_c: Whom having not seen, ye fear and tremble
adults.option_d: Whom not seeing, ye wait with uncertainty
adults.explanation: 1 Peter 1:8 — "Whom having not seen, ye love; in whom, though now ye see him not, yet believing, ye rejoice with joy unspeakable and full of glory."

kids.question: What does Peter call believers?
kids.option_a: A chosen generation and a royal priesthood
kids.explanation: 1 Peter 2:9 — "But ye are a chosen generation, a royal priesthood, an holy nation, a peculiar people."

kids.question: Who is the chief corner stone that Peter talks about?
kids.explanation: 1 Peter 2:6 — "Behold, I lay in Sion a chief corner stone, elect, precious: and he that believeth on him shall not be confounded."

kids.question: What did Jesus do when people said mean things to him?
kids.option_a: He did not answer back with mean words
kids.option_d: He called down fire from heaven
kids.explanation: 1 Peter 2:23 — "Who, when he was reviled, reviled not again; when he suffered, he threatened not."

teens.question: What should believers desire like newborn babies?
teens.option_a: The sincere milk of the word, that they may grow thereby
teens.option_b: Wealth and earthly possessions
teens.option_c: Power and influence in society
teens.option_d: The praise and approval of men
teens.explanation: 1 Peter 2:2 — "As newborn babes, desire the sincere milk of the word, that ye may grow thereby."

teens.question: What are believers called to be built up as?
teens.option_a: A spiritual house and a holy priesthood to offer up spiritual sacrifices
teens.option_b: A physical temple made of stone
teens.option_c: An earthly kingdom with a human king
teens.explanation: 1 Peter 2:5 — "Ye also, as lively stones, are built up a spiritual house, an holy priesthood, to offer up spiritual sacrifices, acceptable to God by Jesus Christ."

teens.question: What does Peter say about submitting to human authorities?
teens.option_a: Submit to every ordinance of man for the Lord's sake
teens.option_b: Rebel against all earthly rulers
teens.option_c: Only obey rulers who are Christians
teens.explanation: 1 Peter 2:13 — "Submit yourselves to every ordinance of man for the Lord's sake: whether it be to the king, as supreme."

teens.question: What happened to the stone the builders disallowed (rejected)?
teens.option_a: It became the head of the corner
teens.option_b: It was thrown away forever
teens.option_c: It was ground into dust
teens.option_d: It was used for a lesser purpose
teens.explanation: 1 Peter 2:7 — "The stone which the builders disallowed, the same is made the head of the corner."

teens.question: By whose stripes are believers healed?
teens.option_a: By the stripes of Christ
teens.option_b: By the stripes of the prophets
teens.option_c: By the stripes of the apostles
teens.explanation: 1 Peter 2:24 — "By whose stripes ye were healed."

adults.question: What does Peter call Christ in verse 4?
adults.option_a: A living stone, disallowed indeed of men, but chosen of God, and precious
adults.option_b: The great high priest after the order of Melchisedec
adults.option_c: The Lion of the tribe of Judah
adults.option_d: The bright and morning star
adults.explanation: 1 Peter 2:4 — "To whom coming, as unto a living stone, disallowed indeed of men, but chosen of God, and precious."

adults.question: What does Christ become to those who are disobedient?
adults.option_a: A stone of stumbling, and a rock of offence
adults.option_b: A light shining in the darkness
adults.option_c: A refuge in the time of storm
adults.option_d: A healer of their wounds
adults.explanation: 1 Peter 2:8 — "And a stone of stumbling, and a rock of offence, even to them which stumble at the word, being disobedient."

adults.question: What four titles does Peter give believers in verse 9?
adults.option_a: A chosen generation, a royal priesthood, an holy nation, a peculiar people
adults.option_b: Apostles, prophets, evangelists, and pastors
adults.option_c: Children, servants, soldiers, and ambassadors
adults.option_d: Priests, Levites, scribes, and Pharisees
adults.explanation: 1 Peter 2:9 — "But ye are a chosen generation, a royal priesthood, an holy nation, a peculiar people; that ye should shew forth the praises of him who hath called you out of darkness into his marvellous light."

adults.question: What does Peter say servants should do regarding their masters?
adults.option_a: Be subject to masters with all fear, not only to the good and gentle but also to the froward
adults.option_b: Only obey kind and fair masters
adults.option_c: Refuse to serve unkind masters
adults.option_d: Flee from harsh masters
adults.explanation: 1 Peter 2:18 — "Servants, be subject to your masters with all fear; not only to the good and gentle, but also to the froward."

adults.question: What is thankworthy (acceptable) in God's sight?
adults.option_a: If a man for conscience toward God endure grief, suffering wrongfully
adults.option_b: If a man accumulates great wealth for the church
adults.option_c: If a man gains political power to spread the gospel
adults.option_d: If a man avoids all suffering through wisdom
adults.explanation: 1 Peter 2:19 — "For this is thankworthy, if a man for conscience toward God endure grief, suffering wrongfully."

adults.question: What does Christ's example teach about suffering?
adults.option_a: When he suffered, he threatened not, but committed himself to him that judgeth righteously
adults.option_b: When he suffered, he called for legions of angels
adults.option_c: When he suffered, he promised vengeance on his enemies
adults.option_d: When he suffered, he cursed those who persecuted him
adults.explanation: 1 Peter 2:23 — "Who, when he was reviled, reviled not again; when he suffered, he threatened not; but committed himself to him that judgeth righteously."

adults.question: What did Christ bear in his own body on the tree?
adults.option_a: Our sins, that we being dead to sins should live unto righteousness
adults.option_b: The curse of the law only
adults.option_c: The punishment for Adam's sin alone
adults.option_d: The weight of the entire world
adults.explanation: 1 Peter 2:24 — "Who his own self bare our sins in his own body on the tree, that we, being dead to sins, should live unto righteousness."

adults.question: What were believers before, but now have returned to whom?
adults.option_a: As sheep going astray, but are now returned unto the Shepherd and Bishop of your souls
adults.option_b: As wolves in sheep's clothing, but now are true believers
adults.option_c: As wandering stars, but now are fixed in heaven
adults.option_d: As lost coins, but now are found by the angels
adults.explanation: 1 Peter 2:25 — "For ye were as sheep going astray; but are now returned unto the Shepherd and Bishop of your souls."

kids.question: What kind of beauty does Peter say is most important?
kids.option_a: The beauty inside your heart — a meek and quiet spirit
kids.explanation: 1 Peter 3:3-4 — "Whose adorning let it not be that outward adorning... But let it be the hidden man of the heart... even the ornament of a meek and quiet spirit."

kids.question: What should we always be ready to do?
kids.option_a: Give an answer about our hope in God
kids.explanation: 1 Peter 3:15 — "Be ready always to give an answer to every man that asketh you a reason of the hope that is in you."

kids.question: What did Jesus do to bring us to God?
kids.option_a: He suffered once for sins, the just for the unjust
kids.option_b: He built a bridge from earth to heaven
kids.option_d: He wrote a letter to God for us
kids.explanation: 1 Peter 3:18 — "For Christ also hath once suffered for sins, the just for the unjust, that he might bring us to God."

teens.question: What does Peter say about outward adornment for women?
teens.option_a: Not the outward adorning of plaiting hair and wearing gold, but the hidden man of the heart
teens.option_b: Always wear the finest clothes to honour God
teens.option_c: Dress as plainly as possible to show humility
teens.option_d: Only wear what the elders approve
teens.explanation: 1 Peter 3:3-4 — "Whose adorning let it not be that outward adorning of plaiting the hair, and of wearing of gold, or of putting on of apparel; But let it be the hidden man of the heart."

teens.question: What does Peter call a meek and quiet spirit?
teens.option_a: Of great price in the sight of God
teens.option_b: Weak and useless in this world
teens.option_c: Acceptable but not necessary
teens.explanation: 1 Peter 3:4 — "Even the ornament of a meek and quiet spirit, which is in the sight of God of great price."

teens.question: What does Peter say husbands should give to their wives?
teens.option_a: Honour, as unto the weaker vessel and as being heirs together of the grace of life
teens.option_b: Commands, as the head of the household
teens.option_c: Permission to speak in the assembly
teens.explanation: 1 Peter 3:7 — "Likewise, ye husbands, dwell with them according to knowledge, giving honour unto the wife, as unto the weaker vessel, and as being heirs together of the grace of life."

teens.question: What should believers not render (give back) for evil?
teens.option_a: Not evil for evil, or railing for railing, but contrariwise blessing
teens.option_b: An equal measure of punishment
teens.option_c: Silence and complete avoidance
teens.explanation: 1 Peter 3:9 — "Not rendering evil for evil, or railing for railing: but contrariwise blessing; knowing that ye are thereunto called, that ye should inherit a blessing."

teens.question: What does Peter say about having a good conscience?
teens.option_a: Having a good conscience so that those who speak evil of you as evildoers may be ashamed
teens.option_b: A good conscience is unnecessary for believers
teens.option_c: A good conscience comes naturally without effort
teens.option_d: A good conscience only matters before God, not men
teens.explanation: 1 Peter 3:16 — "Having a good conscience; that, whereas they speak evil of you, as of evildoers, they may be ashamed that falsely accuse your good conversation in Christ."

adults.question: What Old Testament woman does Peter mention as an example for wives?
adults.option_a: Sara, who obeyed Abraham, calling him lord
adults.option_b: Ruth, who followed Naomi to Bethlehem
adults.option_c: Esther, who saved her people from Haman
adults.option_d: Deborah, who judged Israel
adults.explanation: 1 Peter 3:6 — "Even as Sara obeyed Abraham, calling him lord: whose daughters ye are, as long as ye do well, and are not afraid with any amazement."

adults.question: What does Peter say can be hindered if husbands do not honour their wives?
adults.option_a: Their prayers
adults.option_b: Their income
adults.option_c: Their health
adults.option_d: Their ministry
adults.explanation: 1 Peter 3:7 — "That your prayers be not hindered."

adults.question: How does Peter describe Christ's suffering for sins in verse 18?
adults.option_a: The just for the unjust, that he might bring us to God, being put to death in the flesh but quickened by the Spirit
adults.option_b: As a temporary setback in God's eternal plan
adults.option_c: As an example of passive resistance
adults.option_d: As a political sacrifice for Israel's freedom
adults.explanation: 1 Peter 3:18 — "For Christ also hath once suffered for sins, the just for the unjust, that he might bring us to God, being put to death in the flesh, but quickened by the Spirit."

adults.question: To whom did Christ preach by the Spirit?
adults.option_a: The spirits in prison
adults.option_b: The angels in heaven
adults.option_c: The saints in paradise
adults.option_d: The prophets in the temple
adults.explanation: 1 Peter 3:19 — "By which also he went and preached unto the spirits in prison."

adults.question: When were the spirits in prison disobedient?
adults.option_a: In the days of Noah, while the ark was a preparing
adults.option_b: In the days of Moses at Mount Sinai
adults.option_c: In the days of Abraham in Ur
adults.option_d: In the days of David in Jerusalem
adults.explanation: 1 Peter 3:20 — "Which sometime were disobedient, when once the longsuffering of God waited in the days of Noah, while the ark was a preparing."

adults.question: How does Peter describe the saving work of baptism?
adults.option_a: Not the putting away of the filth of the flesh, but the answer of a good conscience toward God, by the resurrection of Jesus Christ
adults.option_b: The physical washing that cleanses the body
adults.option_c: A ritual that guarantees salvation apart from faith
adults.option_d: A symbolic act with no spiritual significance
adults.explanation: 1 Peter 3:21 — "The like figure whereunto even baptism doth also now save us (not the putting away of the filth of the flesh, but the answer of a good conscience toward God,) by the resurrection of Jesus Christ."

adults.question: What Psalm does Peter quote about the Lord's eyes and ears?
adults.option_a: Psalm 34 — "The eyes of the Lord are over the righteous, and his ears are open unto their prayers"
adults.option_b: Psalm 23 — The Lord is my shepherd
adults.option_c: Psalm 51 — Create in me a clean heart
adults.option_d: Psalm 119 — Thy word is a lamp unto my feet
adults.explanation: 1 Peter 3:12 — "For the eyes of the Lord are over the righteous, and his ears are open unto their prayers: but the face of the Lord is against them that do evil." This quotes Psalm 34:15-16.

adults.question: Where is Christ now according to verse 22?
adults.option_a: At the right hand of God, angels and authorities and powers being made subject unto him
adults.option_b: In the temple in Jerusalem
adults.option_c: Walking among the churches
adults.option_d: Waiting in paradise until the resurrection
adults.explanation: 1 Peter 3:22 — "Who is gone into heaven, and is on the right hand of God; angels and authorities and powers being made subject unto him."

kids.question: What should we use our gifts from God to do?
kids.explanation: 1 Peter 4:10 — "As every man hath received the gift, even so minister the same one to another, as good stewards of the manifold grace of God."

kids.question: If you suffer for being a Christian, should you be ashamed?
kids.option_b: Yes, you should hide your faith
kids.explanation: 1 Peter 4:16 — "Yet if any man suffer as a Christian, let him not be ashamed; but let him glorify God on this behalf."

kids.question: What does Peter say we should have above all things?
kids.option_b: Lots of money in the bank
kids.option_c: The biggest house on the street
kids.explanation: 1 Peter 4:8 — "And above all things have fervent charity among yourselves: for charity shall cover the multitude of sins."

teens.question: What does Peter say about the time past of our lives?
teens.option_a: The time past may suffice us to have wrought the will of the Gentiles
teens.option_b: The past doesn't matter at all anymore
teens.option_c: We should always dwell on our past mistakes
teens.option_d: The past was better than the present
teens.explanation: 1 Peter 4:3 — "For the time past of our life may suffice us to have wrought the will of the Gentiles, when we walked in lasciviousness, lusts, excess of wine, revellings, banquetings, and abominable idolatries."

teens.question: Why do former companions think it strange about believers?
teens.option_a: Because believers no longer run with them to the same excess of riot
teens.option_b: Because believers have become wealthy
teens.option_c: Because believers have moved away
teens.option_d: Because believers speak a different language
teens.explanation: 1 Peter 4:4 — "Wherein they think it strange that ye run not with them to the same excess of riot, speaking evil of you."

teens.question: What does charity (love) cover?
teens.option_a: The multitude of sins
teens.option_b: Only small offences
teens.option_c: Nothing at all
teens.explanation: 1 Peter 4:8 — "For charity shall cover the multitude of sins."

teens.question: What should believers not think strange about?
teens.option_a: The fiery trial which is to try you
teens.option_b: Having money problems
teens.option_c: Making new friends
teens.explanation: 1 Peter 4:12 — "Beloved, think it not strange concerning the fiery trial which is to try you, as though some strange thing happened unto you."

teens.question: What should believers do if they suffer as a Christian?
teens.option_a: Not be ashamed, but glorify God on this behalf
teens.option_b: Fight back against the persecutors
teens.option_c: Deny their faith to avoid trouble
teens.explanation: 1 Peter 4:16 — "Yet if any man suffer as a Christian, let him not be ashamed; but let him glorify God on this behalf."

adults.question: What does Peter mean by "arm yourselves likewise with the same mind"?
adults.option_a: Since Christ suffered in the flesh, believers should adopt the same readiness to suffer, for he that hath suffered in the flesh hath ceased from sin
adults.option_b: Put on physical armor for battle
adults.option_c: Prepare mentally for academic study
adults.option_d: Equip yourselves with earthly weapons
adults.explanation: 1 Peter 4:1 — "Forasmuch then as Christ hath suffered for us in the flesh, arm yourselves likewise with the same mind: for he that hath suffered in the flesh hath ceased from sin."

adults.question: To whom shall the living and dead give account?
adults.option_a: Him that is ready to judge the quick and the dead
adults.option_b: The Roman emperor
adults.option_c: The Jewish Sanhedrin
adults.option_d: The apostles in Jerusalem
adults.explanation: 1 Peter 4:5 — "Who shall give account to him that is ready to judge the quick and the dead."

adults.question: Why was the gospel preached to them that are dead?
adults.option_a: That they might be judged according to men in the flesh, but live according to God in the spirit
adults.option_b: So the dead could convert in the afterlife
adults.option_c: To give the dead a second chance at salvation
adults.option_d: To prove God's power over death
adults.explanation: 1 Peter 4:6 — "For for this cause was the gospel preached also to them that are dead, that they might be judged according to men in the flesh, but live according to God in the spirit."

adults.question: What does Peter say about the end of all things?
adults.option_a: The end of all things is at hand; be ye therefore sober, and watch unto prayer
adults.option_b: The end is far away, so relax
adults.option_c: The end has already passed
adults.option_d: The end cannot be known by anyone
adults.explanation: 1 Peter 4:7 — "But the end of all things is at hand: be ye therefore sober, and watch unto prayer."

adults.question: How should those who speak in ministry speak?
adults.option_a: As the oracles of God
adults.option_b: As the philosophers of Greece
adults.option_c: As the scribes of Israel
adults.option_d: As the senators of Rome
adults.explanation: 1 Peter 4:11 — "If any man speak, let him speak as the oracles of God."

adults.question: Where does Peter say judgment must begin?
adults.option_a: At the house of God
adults.option_b: At the throne of Caesar
adults.option_c: At the gates of Jerusalem
adults.option_d: At the temple of Diana
adults.explanation: 1 Peter 4:17 — "For the time is come that judgment must begin at the house of God: and if it first begin at us, what shall the end be of them that obey not the gospel of God?"

adults.question: If the righteous scarcely be saved, what question does Peter raise?
adults.option_a: Where shall the ungodly and the sinner appear?
adults.option_b: How shall the righteous be rewarded?
adults.option_c: When shall the prophets return?
adults.option_d: Why shall the wicked prosper?
adults.explanation: 1 Peter 4:18 — "And if the righteous scarcely be saved, where shall the ungodly and the sinner appear?"

adults.question: What should those who suffer according to the will of God do?
adults.option_a: Commit the keeping of their souls to him in well doing, as unto a faithful Creator
adults.option_b: Demand immediate vindication from God
adults.option_c: Withdraw from all social contact
adults.option_d: Seek revenge when the opportunity arises
adults.explanation: 1 Peter 4:19 — "Wherefore let them that suffer according to the will of God commit the keeping of their souls to him in well doing, as unto a faithful Creator."

kids.question: Who does Peter tell to feed (take care of) the flock of God?
kids.explanation: 1 Peter 5:1-2 — "The elders which are among you I exhort... Feed the flock of God which is among you."

kids.question: What animal does Peter compare the devil to?
kids.explanation: 1 Peter 5:8 — "Your adversary the devil, as a roaring lion, walketh about, seeking whom he may devour."

kids.question: What should you do with your worries?
kids.option_a: Give them to God because he cares for you
kids.explanation: 1 Peter 5:7 — "Casting all your care upon him; for he careth for you."

teens.question: How should elders shepherd the flock?
teens.option_a: Not by constraint but willingly; not for filthy lucre but of a ready mind
teens.option_b: By strict rules and heavy penalties
teens.option_c: By hiring others to do the work
teens.explanation: 1 Peter 5:2 — "Feed the flock of God which is among you, taking the oversight thereof, not by constraint, but willingly; not for filthy lucre, but of a ready mind."

teens.question: What does Peter say younger people should do?
teens.option_a: Submit to the elder, and all be clothed with humility
teens.option_b: Challenge the elders at every opportunity
teens.option_c: Ignore the elders and follow their own plans
teens.option_d: Take leadership from the elders by force
teens.explanation: 1 Peter 5:5 — "Likewise, ye younger, submit yourselves unto the elder. Yea, all of you be subject one to another, and be clothed with humility."

teens.question: What does God give to the humble?
teens.option_a: Grace — God resisteth the proud and giveth grace to the humble
teens.option_b: Wealth and power beyond measure
teens.option_c: Freedom from all suffering
teens.option_d: Angels to serve their every need
teens.explanation: 1 Peter 5:5 — "God resisteth the proud, and giveth grace to the humble."

teens.question: What does Peter say to do under God's mighty hand?
teens.option_a: Humble yourselves, that he may exalt you in due time
teens.option_b: Stand tall and demand your rights
teens.option_c: Complain until God blesses you
teens.option_d: Do nothing and wait for a miracle
teens.explanation: 1 Peter 5:6 — "Humble yourselves therefore under the mighty hand of God, that he may exalt you in due time."

teens.question: Who does Peter say helped him write this letter?
teens.option_a: Silvanus, a faithful brother
teens.option_b: Timothy, a young pastor
teens.option_c: Luke, a beloved physician
teens.explanation: 1 Peter 5:12 — "By Silvanus, a faithful brother unto you, as I suppose, I have written briefly."

adults.question: What does Peter call himself among the elders?
adults.option_a: A fellow elder and witness of the sufferings of Christ, and a partaker of the glory that shall be revealed
adults.option_b: The chief apostle over all elders
adults.option_c: The bishop of Rome appointed by Christ
adults.option_d: The greatest of all the apostles
adults.explanation: 1 Peter 5:1 — "The elders which are among you I exhort, who am also an elder, and a witness of the sufferings of Christ, and also a partaker of the glory that shall be revealed."

adults.question: What should elders not be?
adults.option_a: Lords over God's heritage, but ensamples to the flock
adults.option_b: Friends with the congregation
adults.option_c: Gentle and patient with sinners
adults.option_d: Willing to serve without pay
adults.explanation: 1 Peter 5:3 — "Neither as being lords over God's heritage, but being ensamples to the flock."

adults.question: What will the chief Shepherd give when he shall appear?
adults.option_a: A crown of glory that fadeth not away
adults.option_b: A crown of thorns to share in his suffering
adults.option_c: A golden scepter of earthly authority
adults.option_d: A mansion in the heavenly city
adults.explanation: 1 Peter 5:4 — "And when the chief Shepherd shall appear, ye shall receive a crown of glory that fadeth not away."

adults.question: After believers have suffered a while, what does the God of all grace do?
adults.option_a: Make you perfect, stablish, strengthen, settle you
adults.option_b: Remove all suffering permanently
adults.option_c: Grant immediate revenge on enemies
adults.option_d: Transport them directly to heaven
adults.explanation: 1 Peter 5:10 — "But the God of all grace, who hath called us unto his eternal glory by Christ Jesus, after that ye have suffered a while, make you perfect, stablish, strengthen, settle you."

adults.question: What does Peter call "Babylon" in his closing greetings?
adults.option_a: The church that is at Babylon, elected together with you — widely understood as a reference to Rome
adults.option_b: A literal city in Mesopotamia
adults.option_c: A small village near Jerusalem
adults.option_d: An imaginary place representing heaven
adults.explanation: 1 Peter 5:13 — "The church that is at Babylon, elected together with you, saluteth you." Most scholars understand "Babylon" as a cryptic reference to Rome.

adults.question: Who does Peter mention as greeting the readers along with the church?
adults.option_a: Marcus my son — referring to John Mark
adults.option_b: Timothy my true son in the faith
adults.option_c: Luke the beloved physician
adults.option_d: Apollos the eloquent preacher
adults.explanation: 1 Peter 5:13 — "And so doth Marcus my son."

adults.question: How does Peter describe Silvanus in verse 12?
adults.option_a: A faithful brother
adults.option_b: A mighty prophet
adults.option_c: A skilled physician
adults.option_d: A wealthy supporter
adults.explanation: 1 Peter 5:12 — "By Silvanus, a faithful brother unto you, as I suppose, I have written briefly."

adults.question: What does Peter say is the true grace of God in verse 12?
adults.option_a: This is the true grace of God wherein ye stand
adults.option_b: The ability to speak in tongues
adults.option_c: The power to perform miracles
adults.option_d: The gift of prophetic visions
adults.explanation: 1 Peter 5:12 — "Exhorting, and testifying that this is the true grace of God wherein ye stand."
